step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the initial conclusion. Another important feature supporting this diagnosis is the presence of cutaneous lesions typically involving the forehead and scalp, often associated with leptomeningeal angiomatosis—a defining characteristic of Sturge-Weber syndrome not commonly observed in other listed options.
raw_response:
I agree with the initial conclusion. Another important feature supporting this diagnosis is the presence of cutaneous lesions typically involving the forehead and scalp, often associated with leptomeningeal angiomatosis—a defining characteristic of Sturge-Weber syndrome not commonly observed in other listed options.

Answer: A. Sturge Weber syndrome
